response: {"purpose":"Figure 3c compares the fraction of usable (non-PCR duplicate) reads out of all uniquely mapped reads across three CLIP-based methodologies: eCLIP, iCLIP (both ENCODE consortium and published datasets), and published CLIP datasets (including PAR-CLIP and HITS-CLIP). By displaying individual data points overlaid with kernel density-smoothed histograms, the panel allows direct visual comparison of library complexity and sequencing efficiency across a large number of experiments. This comparison matters because PCR duplication is a major source of noise and inefficiency in CLIP protocols, and demonstrating that eCLIP produces a higher fraction of usable reads substantiates its scalability and data quality advantages over existing methods. Establishing superior library complexity is critical for justifying eCLIP as the preferred approach for large-scale RBP target identification efforts such as those undertaken by the ENCODE consortium.","title":"eCLIP Yields Higher Usable Read Fractions Than iCLIP and CLIP"}
